sentences: The decoder of the <ENT_008> takes object queries as input that are responsible for object detection in various spatial locations.
These object queries combine with spatial features from the encoder.
The co-attention mechanism in <ENT_008> involves computing a set of attention maps between the object queries and the image features to provide class labels and bounding box locations.
However, the visual regions in the decoder of <ENT_008> related to object query might be irrelevant to the predicted bounding boxes.
This is one of the reasons that <ENT_008> needs many training epochs to find suitable visual locations to identify corresponding objects correctly.

<ENT_073> et al. <REF_012> introduced a spatially modulated coattention (<ENT_072>) module that replaces the existing co-attention mechanism in <ENT_008> to overcome the slow training convergence of <ENT_008>.
The object queries estimate the scale and center of its corresponding object, which are further used to set up a 2D spatial weight map.
The initial estimate of scale <MATH_065> and center <MATH_066> of a Gaussian-like distribution for object queries q is provided as follows:
